target.name: DeepDrive
target.founder: Felix Pörnbacher, Alexander Rosen, Markus Domme, Christopher Rommelmayer, Maximilian Habersbrunner, Dean Petrovski, Stefan Ender

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $54M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 3 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
DeepDrive is a Series B transportation company that has raised $54.4M to date. The transportation sector comprises seven private peers of varying stage and scale, including Rivian, The Boring Company, and Einride; no public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

Stage-progression analysis is the applicable valuation method here. Using total capital raised ($54.4M) as a proxy for Series B valuation and applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B-to-later-stage progression yields $103M. This method captures the expected value increase from capital deployment and operational milestones between funding rounds. Peer-median analysis was not applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the Transportation sector—only three peers exist overall, none meeting the minimum threshold of three datapoints in narrow peer filters.

The triangulated valuation range is $88M to $119M, with a median of $103M. This single-method result should be treated with appropriate caution given reliance on a stage-progression heuristic rather than multiple triangulation points. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 1.9x multiplier to DeepDrive's specific risk profile, capital efficiency, and market conditions relative to the broader peer set. A more robust valuation would benefit from expanded peer data or additional methodologies (revenue-multiple, DCF) once operational metrics become available.
